comment0: Metadata.json - Insert the e-book's metadata here, so that the content.opf and toc.ncx files can be filled. May use data for ONIX too. WIP
comment1: Folder metadata - This contains the names of the technical folders, if you change the default names please update them here.
comment2: Technical metadata - This is the where the cover image is specified. Recommended to use current EPUB v3.3 over legacy v2.0.1. Reflowable rather than Fixed for layout (formerly textPresentation) - unless doing a project that requires a specific layout which uses 'width' and 'height' key-value pairs for the page dimensions. Enable NCX for backward compatibility with EPUB 2.0.1. The 'lastModified' date is an autogenerated timestamp applied to the Content.opf that can be used for ARC leak verification amongst other things. The 'compressionLevel' is experimental and for the level of compression which should make the EPUB smaller but still readable.
comment3: Minification - Minifying the files removes whitespace from the copied files and makes the resulting EPUB file smaller. These options are modular and can remove unused CSS classes and comments to trim the CSS to prevent reverse engineering. Also, a copyright notice can be put on the first line of each file. This section is optional and turned off by default.
copyrightNotice:: Copyright (C) 2025 Motley Press. All Rights Reserved.
comment4: Fingerprinting - Fingerprinting are techniques used to uniquely identify the e-book. Checksums are MD5, SHA-256 and SHA-512 which are generated and stored as checksums.txt.
comment5: Book metadata - Information about the e-book itself. Language is specified with ISO 639-1. ISBN number can be declared, as well as the Amazon ASIN. Rights can be worldwide, country specific or under a permissable license such as Creative-Commons SA. Contributors are referenced using the MARC standard (aut = author, edt = editor) via https://www.loc.gov/marc/relators/relaterm.html. Date is the publication date and follows iso8601-1 as recommended by W3C.
title: Skyrim Library Unofficial
subject: fantasy
comment6: This specifies the tags used by Amazon Kindle's start reading, the table of contents and the front cover using the <guide></guide> tags. Optional and enable it.
comment7: This is the page order of the e-book that forms the basis of the toc.ncx and the nav.xhtml.
